content.full_text:
Glen H. Spain, Inc.
Professional Law Corporation

Phone 937-2324
Area Code 707

P.O. Box 205
Comptche, CA 95427

Jun 14 9 35 AM '90

June 11, 1990

Fair Political Practices Commission
Legal Opinion/Technican Assistance
428 J Street, Suit 800
P.O. Box 807
SACRAMENTO, CA 95804

RE: Request for formal Opinion Letter and/or Advice Letter
pursuant to Gov. C. 83114

To the Examining Officer:

This letter is a request for a formal opinion with respect to the
question contained herein, pursuant to Government Code 83114. In the event
that an Opinion Letter cannot be issued, this letter is in the alternative
also a request for an Advice Letter with respect to this question which can
be relied upon as a complete defense in any enforcement proceeding
initiated by the Commission.

Relevant Facts: I represent Recipient Committee #891518, whose full
name is "Forests Forever, Committee for Proposition 30". This entity is a
sponsored Committee of the nonprofit corporation entitled "Forests
Forever", which is a California nonprofit corporation (Corporate Number
1653374) and which is tax exempt under state and federal law (under R. &
T.C. 23701f & I.R.C 501(c)(4) respectively). I also am representing this
corporation in making this request.

In addition, there exists another Recipient Committee (#900423)
entitled "Forests Forever Committee, A Coalition of Conservation
Organizations" which is in the process of incorporation and amending its
registration to the name "The Forest and Wildlife Protection Committee".
This latter name will be the name by which it is referred to in the
following.

Both Committees are organized to support the Forest and Wildlife
Protection and Bond Act of 1990, a statewide ballot measure which will be
on the ballot on November 6, 1990. Although legally and financially
separate, both are engaging in joint fundraising and cooperating in the
campaign to pass that measure.

After the November 6th, 1990, election it is anticipated that both
Committees will terminate. After the campaign, and after the payment of
all campaign expenses there will probably be excess campaign funds which
will have to then be distributed. Both Committees will at that time seek
to distribute those excess campaign funds to Forests Forever corporation,
pursuant to Government Code 85803, as donations to a "tax-exempt, nonprofit
organization".

-1-

Fair Political Practices Commission
RE: Forests Forever corporation
June 11, 1990


The organizational purposes of Forests Forever corporation, as stated
in its Articles of Incorporation, are as follows:

"ARTICLE 3: The specific purposes for which the corporation is
organized are:

(a) to produce and distribute educational material in the public
interest, including (but not limited to) material concerning the
conservation, restoration, wise use and set-asides of the Earth's
natural resources with full-cost environmental accounting for the
long-term continuation of both biological functions and employment,
as they are interrelated;

(b) to qualify and effect California statewide ballot initiatives,
conduct campaigns in support of statewide Propositions (or other
local citizen's initiatives) and to serve as a "Recipient
Committee" as defined in California Government Code Section 82013
for the purposes of raising funds for the financial support of
those campaigns;

(c) to establish offices in the State of California where it may
contract services and receive donations or loans and other
financial assistance to be used to fulfill the purposes of the
corporation;

(d) to exercise all of the general powers permitted to a California
nonprofit Public Benefit Corporation under California law, insofar
as those powers are consistent with these Articles of
Incorporation."

The original form of the measure was written and sponsored by Forests
Forever prior to its incorporation, and the corporation expects to either
take an active role in enforcing its provisions (if approved by the voters)
or alternatively to make efforts to implement its provisions by way of
various administrative agencies or other statewide ballot measures. The
corporation's efforts are directly related to the purposes for which the
measure was originally proposed and which it is intended to effect.


-2-

Fair Political Practices Commission
RE: Forests Forever corporation
June 11, 1990

The current corporate Treasurer is also the Treasurer of "Forests
Forever, Committee for Proposition 30", and he will be on the Board of
Directors of the corporation in the near future. Though his services as
Treasurer may be modestly compensated, in no case will such compensation
ever exceed what is fair and reasonable for actual services rendered. His
compensation is set by vote of the Board of Directors of the corporation
(himself excluded if serving as a Director) and would be a fixed amount
regardless of whether or not excess campaign funds are received. The
corporation will have a number of other sources of income in addition to
excess campaign funds, if any.

Directors of the corporation are not compensated as Directors. The
Articles of Incorporation also contain the following restriction:

"ARTICLE 6: The property of this corporation is irrevocably dedicated to
social welfare purposes and no part of the net income or assets of this
corporation shall ever inure to the benefit of any director, officer or
member thereof or to the benefit of any private person."

Thus the distribution of excess campaign funds to Forests Forever
corporation will not have a material financial effect on the campaign
Treasurer and will bear a reasonable relation to the political, legislative
and governmental purposes for which the measure was proposed and supported.

The dissolution clause in the Articles of Incorporation of the
recently incorporated Forest and Wildlife Protection Committee allows
distribution of all its assets, upon winding up and dissolution, to a
501(c)(4) organization such as Forests Forever.

QUESTION: We therefore request an Opinion Letter and/or Advice Letter
as to the following question, based on the above information:

"After the campaigns for which they are organized, and after the
payment of all campaign expenses, can Recipient Committee No. 891518
(Forests Forever, Committee for Proposition 30) and Recipient
Committee No. 900423 (The Forest and Wildlife Protection Committee)
then distribute their excess campaign funds to Forests Forever, a
tax-exempt and nonprofit California corporation?"

If any additional information is required before you can complete your
written response, please feel free to contact me. Otherwise we look
forward to your response to the above question in the near future.

SINCERELY,

GLEN H. SPAIN
ATTORNEY AT LAW

GHS/lt
Cc: Forests Forever
    (Day, Gregory, Lanman)

    Wes Van Winkle                                                    -3-
